NEG/LEARNING cycle334 (feat/cudss-kernel-tuning): two more ncu-guided micro-opts, both reverted. (a) Removed the redundant 2nd __syncthreads in the multi-warp backward path (the cross-warp wsum-sum and the back-solve are both thread-0-only with no later reader) -> NEUTRAL (<0.5%); the barrier was near-free because by that point the other warps have exited. Hoisting the nc pivots into a register array piv[8] -> REGRESSED +1.6..+4.3% ALL matrices. CAUSE: the extra 16 regs/thread cut occupancy on the well-populated forward leaf levels, and that occupancy was HIDING the shared-scoreboard latency across fronts. KEY LEARNING: forward and backward have OPPOSITE occupancy regimes -- forward leaf = many fronts, latency hidden by occupancy (do NOT add register pressure); backward spine = one front/level, latency EXPOSED (remove overhead, cy332/333 wins). ncu's per-kernel stall%% does not capture the cross-front occupancy benefit, so trust the wall-clock A/B over the stall%%. Source reverted to cy333; gpu_test 4/4.
